# Ben & Jerry's Ice Cream Dataframe Analysis

### Data Loading







Let's analyze the `beer_data` data:



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ice_cream <- read_csv("https://bcdanl.github.io/data/ben-and-jerry-cleaned.csv")
```
:::



## Summary of Data

This code provides the following statistics:

-   Mean (price)

-   Median (price)

-   Standard Deviation (price)

-   Mean (Coupon)

-   Mean (Household size)




```{.r .cell-code}
mySummary <- ice_cream %>%
  summarise(
    mean_price = mean(priceper1),
    median_price = median(priceper1),
    sd_price = sd(priceper1),
    avg_coupon = mean(couponper1),
    avg_household_size = mean(household_size)
  )
data.frame(mySummary)
```

  mean_price median_price  sd_price avg_coupon avg_household_size
1   3.314627         3.34 0.6656264  0.1255788           2.456403



### Top 5 Ben & Jerry's **Flavors**



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ice_cream %>% 
  count(ice_cream$flavor_descr) %>% 
  arrange(-n) %>% 
  head(10)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 10 × 2
   `ice_cream$flavor_descr`       n
   <chr>                      <int>
 1 CHERRY GRCA                 2097
 2 CHC FUDGE BROWNIE           1235

 6 PHISH FOOD                   968
 7 NEW YORK SUPER FUDGE CHUNK   932
 8 AMERICONE DREAM              865
 9 PB CUP                       828
10 KARAMEL SUTRA                738
```


:::
:::



## Regional DataGrouping



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
regional_ice_cream <- ice_cream %>% 
  count(region, flavor_descr, name = "n") %>% 
  group_by(region) %>% 
  slice_max(n, n = 5)

regional_ice_cream
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 20 × 3
# Groups:   region [4]
   region  flavor_descr                   n
   <chr>   <chr>                      <int>

12 South   CHC FUDGE BROWNIE            496
```

:::
:::



## Plotting



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(regional_ice_cream,
       aes(
         x = flavor_descr,
         y = n,
         color = region
       )) +
  geom_point(show.legend = F) + 
  facet_wrap(~ region, ncol = 4) + 
  ggthemes::theme_fivethirtyeight() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(title = "Regional Best Sellers")
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Ben-and-Jerrys_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-6-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::



## Regression Model



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
model <- lm(priceper1 ~ couponper1 + household_income +
              household_size + region,
            data = ice_cream)

unique(ice_cream$household_income)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
 [1] 130000  70000 110000 210000 190000  80000  40000 150000 160000  50000
[11] 180000 170000  60000 280000 310000 240000 230000 300000 260000
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
library(stargazer)
stargazer(model, type = 'text')
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```

===============================================
                        Dependent variable:    
                    ---------------------------
                             priceper1         
-----------------------------------------------
couponper1                   0.208***          
                              (0.009)          
                                               
household_income            -0.00000***        
                             (0.00000)         
                                               
household_size               -0.029***         
                              (0.003)          
                                               
regionEast                   0.142***          
                              (0.013)          
                                               
regionSouth                   -0.011           
                              (0.012)          
                                               
regionWest                   0.048***          
                              (0.013)          
                                               
Constant                     3.424***          
                              (0.017)          
                                               
-----------------------------------------------
Observations                  21,974           
R2                             0.041           
Adjusted R2                    0.041           
Residual Std. Error     0.652 (df = 21967)     
F Statistic         157.045*** (df = 6; 21967) 
===============================================
Note:               *p<0.1; **p<0.05; ***p<0.01
```


:::
:::



### Explanation

Of this linear regression model, we can see that all except the south region is statistically significant. Another indicator we can use to help make valuable assumptions is the R\^2 value, where it is fairly low for this model, but that doesn't necessarily mean there's no correlation. It also seems that the household income has a very minimal effect, with the dependent variable being -0.00000. After researching possible reasons to this, it was concluded that the values within that column of data might be too large, and would have to be scaled back in order to get a visible number, so that it isn't cut off.
